description: city in and county seat of Dubuque, Iowa, United States
description_wiki: Dubuque ( , dəb-YOOK) is a city in Dubuque County, Iowa, United States, and its county seat.

description: unincorporated community in Grant County, Wisconsin
description_wiki: Louisburg is an unincorporated community in Grant County, Wisconsin, United States, located within the town of Jamestown.

description: village in Grant County, Wisconsin
description_wiki: Hazel Green is a village in Grant and Lafayette counties in the U.S. state of Wisconsin.

surface_forms: Southwestern High School in Hazel Green, Wisconsin

description: Wikimedia disambiguation page
description_wiki: Little Shop of Horrors may refer to:

description: television series
description_wiki: L.A. Law is an American legal drama television series created by Steven Bochco and Terry Louise Fisher for NBC. It ran for eight seasons and 172 episodes from September 15, 1986, to May 19, 1994.

description: American legal drama television series (1995-2005)
description_wiki: JAG  (U.S. military acronym for Judge Advocate General) is an American legal drama television series with a U.S. Navy theme, created by Donald P. Bellisario and produced by Belisarius Productions in association with Paramount Network Television (now CBS Studios).

description: component of the US Army and military of the U.S. state of Wisconsin
description_wiki: The Wisconsin Army National Guard has dual state and federal roles, and is jointly funded and maintained by both governments.

description: public university in Cedar Falls, Iowa
description_wiki: The University of Northern Iowa (UNI) is a public university in Cedar Falls, Iowa, United States.

description: 1950 battle in the Korean War
description_wiki: The Battle of Chosin Reservoir, also known as the Chosin Reservoir Campaign or the Battle of Lake Changjin (Korean: 장진호 전투; Hanja: 長津湖戰鬪; RR: Jangjinho jeontu; MR: Changjinho chŏnt'u), was a battle in the Korean War.

description: active United States Army formation
description_wiki: The 101st Airborne Division (Air Assault) ("Screaming Eagles") is a light infantry division of the United States Army that specializes in air assault operations.

description: active United States Army formation
description_wiki: The 82nd Airborne Division ("All American")  is a United States Army airborne infantry division that specializes in parachute assault, based in Fort Bragg, North Carolina.

description: 1939–1945 global conflict
description_wiki: World War II, or the Second World War (1 September 1939 – 2 September 1945), was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers.

description: 1944-1945 World War II battle
description_wiki: The Battle of the Bulge, also known as the Ardennes Offensive, was the last major German offensive campaign on the Western Front during the Second World War, taking place from 16 December 1944 to 25 January 1945.

surface_forms: old M44, the German machine gun

description: 2001 attacks in the United States
description_wiki: The September 11 attacks, colloquially known as 9/11, were a coordinated series of suicide attacks perpetrated by the Islamist terrorist organization al-Qaeda against the United States in 2001.

description: capital city of the United States of America
description_wiki: Washington, D.C., officially the District of Columbia and commonly known as simply Washington or D.C., is the capital city and federal district of the United States.

description: county in Virginia, United States; part of Washington metropolitan area
description_wiki: Arlington County, or simply Arlington, is a county in the U.S. state of Virginia.

aliases: Headquarters of the United States Department of Defense
Headquarters of the United States Department of War (2025–present)
Headquarters of the United States Department of Defense (1947–2025)
Headquarters of the United States Department of War (1789–1947)
description: headquarters of the United States Department of Defense; named after the shape of the building, a polygon with five (greek: penta) sides
description_wiki: The Pentagon is the headquarters of the United States Department of Defense, in Arlington County, Virginia, across the Potomac River from Washington, D.C. The building was constructed on an accelerated schedule during World War II. As a symbol of the U.S. military, the phrase The Pentagon is often used as a metonym for the Department of Defense and its leadership.

description: private university in Washington, D.C., USA
description_wiki: The George Washington University (GW or GWU) is a private federally chartered research university in Washington, D.C., United States.

description: United States Army installation and a CDP in Fairfax County, Virginia, United States
description_wiki: Fort Belvoir ( BEL-vwar) is a United States Army installation and a census-designated place (CDP) in Fairfax County, Virginia, United States.

description: top institution of the U.S. Republican Party
description_wiki: The Republican National Committee (RNC) is the primary committee of the Republican Party of the United States.

description: 44th Governor of Wisconsin
description_wiki: James Edward Doyle Jr.

description: state capitol building of the U.S. state of Wisconsin
description_wiki: The Wisconsin State Capitol, located in Madison, Wisconsin, houses both chambers of the Wisconsin Legislature along with the Wisconsin Supreme Court and the Office of the Governor.

description: military unit
federal reserve force of the United States Army
description_wiki: The United States Army Reserve (USAR) is a reserve force of the United States Army.

description: historic fort in Minnesota near the confluence of the Mississippi and Minnesota River
description_wiki: Fort Snelling is a former military fortification and National Historic Landmark in the U.S. state of Minnesota on the bluffs overlooking the confluence of the Minnesota and Mississippi Rivers.

description: formation of the United States Army (1941–1992)
description_wiki: The 3rd Armored Division (also known as "Spearhead", 3rd Armored, and 3AD) was an armored division of the United States Army.

description: country in West Asia and Southeast Europe
description_wiki: Turkey, officially the Republic of Türkiye, is a country mainly located in Anatolia in West Asia, with a smaller part called East Thrace in Southeast Europe.

description: active United States Army formation
description_wiki: The 4th Infantry Division is a division of the United States Army based at Fort Carson, Colorado.

description: sovereign state in Western Asia
description_wiki: Kuwait, officially the State of Kuwait, is a country in West Asia.

description: city in Saladin, Iraq
description_wiki: Tikrit (Arabic: تِكْرِيت, romanized: Tikrīt [ˈtɪkriːt]) is a city in Iraq, located 140 kilometers (87 mi) northwest of Baghdad and 220 kilometers (140 mi) southeast of Mosul on the Tigris River.

description: aerial warfare branch of Iraq's armed forces
description_wiki: The Iraqi Air Force (IQAF; Arabic: القوة الجوية العراقية, romanized: al-Qūwah al-jawwīyah al-ʻIrāqīyah) is the aerial warfare service branch of the Iraqi Armed Forces.
